openalex.title: The hot circumgalactic medium in the eROSITA All-Sky Survey
openalex.display_name: The hot circumgalactic medium in the eROSITA All-Sky Survey

crossref.abstract: <jats:p><jats:italic>Context.</jats:italic>The circumgalactic medium (CGM) provides the material needed for galaxy formation and influences galaxy evolution. The hot (<jats:italic>T</jats:italic> > 10<jats:sup>6</jats:sup><jats:italic>K</jats:italic>) CGM is poorly detected around galaxies with stellar masses (<jats:italic>M</jats:italic><jats:sub>*</jats:sub>) lower than 3 × 10<jats:sup>11</jats:sup> <jats:italic>M</jats:italic><jats:sub>⊙</jats:sub>due to the low surface brightness.</jats:p><jats:p><jats:italic>Aims.</jats:italic>We aim to detect the X-ray emission from the hot CGM around Milky Way-mass (MW-mass, log(<jats:italic>M</jats:italic><jats:sub>*</jats:sub>/<jats:italic>M</jats:italic><jats:sub>⊙</jats:sub>) = 10.5 − 11.0) and M31-mass (log(<jats:italic>M</jats:italic><jats:sub>*</jats:sub>/<jats:italic>M</jats:italic><jats:sub>⊙</jats:sub>) = 11.0 − 11.25) galaxies, in addition to measuring the X-ray surface brightness profile of the hot CGM.</jats:p><jats:p><jats:italic>Methods.</jats:italic>We applied a stacking technique to gain enough statistics to detect the hot CGM. We used the X-ray data from the first four SRG/eROSITA All-Sky Surveys (eRASS:4). We discussed how the satellite galaxies could bias the stacking and the method we used to carefully build the central galaxy samples. Based on the SDSS spectroscopic survey and halo-based group finder algorithm, we selected central galaxies with spectroscopic redshifts of<jats:italic>z</jats:italic><jats:sub>spec</jats:sub> < 0.2 and stellar masses of 10.0 < log(<jats:italic>M</jats:italic><jats:sub>*</jats:sub>/<jats:italic>M</jats:italic><jats:sub>⊙</jats:sub>) < 11.5 (85 222 galaxies) – or halo masses of 11.5 < log(<jats:italic>M</jats:italic><jats:sub>200m</jats:sub>/<jats:italic>M</jats:italic><jats:sub>⊙</jats:sub>) < 14.0 (125,512 galaxies). By stacking the X-ray emission around galaxies, we obtained the mean X-ray surface brightness profiles. We masked the detected X-ray point sources and carefully modeled the X-ray emission from the unresolved active galactic nuclei (AGN) and X-ray binaries (XRB) to obtain the X-ray emission from the hot CGM.</jats:p><jats:p><jats:italic>Results.</jats:italic>We measured the X-ray surface brightness profiles for central galaxies of log(<jats:italic>M</jats:italic><jats:sub>*</jats:sub>/<jats:italic>M</jats:italic><jats:sub>⊙</jats:sub>) > 10.0 or log(<jats:italic>M</jats:italic><jats:sub>200m</jats:sub>/<jats:italic>M</jats:italic><jats:sub>⊙</jats:sub>) > 11.5. We detected the X-ray emission around MW-mass and more massive central galaxies extending up to the virial radius (<jats:italic>R</jats:italic><jats:sub>vir</jats:sub>). The signal-to-noise ratio (S/N) of the extended emission around MW-mass (M31-mass) galaxy is about 3.1<jats:italic>σ</jats:italic>(4.7<jats:italic>σ</jats:italic>) within<jats:italic>R</jats:italic><jats:sub>vir</jats:sub>. We used a<jats:italic>β</jats:italic>model to describe the X-ray surface brightness profile of the hot CGM (<jats:italic>S</jats:italic><jats:sub>X, CGM</jats:sub>). We obtained a central surface brightness of log(<jats:italic>S</jats:italic><jats:sub>X,0</jats:sub>[erg s<jats:sup>−1</jats:sup> kpc<jats:sup>−2</jats:sup>]) = 36.7<jats:sub>−0.4</jats:sub><jats:sup>+1.4</jats:sup> (37.1<jats:sub>−0.4</jats:sub><jats:sup>+1.5</jats:sup>) and<jats:italic>β</jats:italic> = 0.43<jats:sub>−0.06</jats:sub><jats:sup>+0.10</jats:sup> (0.37<jats:sub>−0.02</jats:sub><jats:sup>+0.04</jats:sup>) for MW-mass (M31-mass) galaxies. For galaxies with log(<jats:italic>M</jats:italic><jats:sub>200m</jats:sub>/<jats:italic>M</jats:italic><jats:sub>⊙</jats:sub>) > 12.5, the extended X-ray emission is detected with S/N > 2.8<jats:italic>σ</jats:italic>and the<jats:italic>S</jats:italic><jats:sub>X, CGM</jats:sub>can be described by a<jats:italic>β</jats:italic>model with<jats:italic>β</jats:italic> ≈ 0.4 and log(<jats:italic>S</jats:italic><jats:sub>X,0</jats:sub>[erg s<jats:sup>−1</jats:sup> kpc<jats:sup>−2</jats:sup>]) > 37.2. We estimated the baryon budget of the hot CGM and obtained a value that is lower than the prediction of ΛCDM cosmology, indicating significant gas depletion in these halos. We extrapolated the hot CGM profile measured within<jats:italic>R</jats:italic><jats:sub>vir</jats:sub>to larger radii and found that within ≈3<jats:italic>R</jats:italic><jats:sub>vir</jats:sub>, the baryon budget is close to the ΛCDM cosmology prediction.</jats:p><jats:p><jats:italic>Conclusions.</jats:italic>We measured the extended X-ray emission from representative populations of central galaxies around and above MW-mass out to<jats:italic>R</jats:italic><jats:sub>vir</jats:sub>. Our results set a firm footing for the presence of the hot CGM around such galaxies. These measurements constitute a new benchmark for galaxy evolution models and possible implementations of feedback processes therein.</jats:p>
title: The hot circumgalactic medium in the eROSITA All-Sky Survey

Hogg D. W., Bovy J., & Lang D. 2010, ArXiv e-prints [arXiv:1008.4686]
Laureijs R., Amiaux J., Arduini S., et al. 2011, ArXiv e-prints [arXiv:1110.3193]
Merloni A., Predehl P., Becker W., et al. 2012, ArXiv e-prints [arXiv:1209.3114]
Nandra K., Barret D., Barcons X., et al. 2013, ArXiv e-prints [arXiv:1306.2307]
Onken C. A., Wolf C., Bessell M. S., et al. 2019, PASA, 36

subtitle: I. X-ray surface brightness profiles
